author: wddgit
body:
#### PR description:

Reduce the memory usage of the ```InputFileCatalog```.

Before this PR, ```InputFileCatalog``` would calculate all the PFNs in its constructor and hold them in memory for the entire job. This PR eliminates that. Only the configured file names are saved in memory for the entire job. The PFNs are calculated when needed and returned to the caller that asks for them. ```InputFileCatalog``` does not save them anymore.

In jobs where the number of input files is small the savings are negligible. But in some pileup jobs this can save hundreds of megabytes of memory. There is history here. The memory problem was originally discovered and documented in Issue #46975. This PR implements changes proposed in Issue #47050. PR #47013  was merged more than a year ago and made significant memory improvements to InputFileCatalog  that were different from what is done here and orthogonal to these changes. PR #49430 and PR #49134 also made improvements to InputFileCatalog to prepare for this PR. 

There is still one more significant improvement that needs to made in the future after this PR. We currently replicate ```InputFileCatalog```, one for each Stream. The class is thread safe and there is no reason for that replication. One object should be shared by many streams.

The intent of this PR is that the output of processing is not changed and is exactly the same. The only noticeable difference should be in memory used.

Resolves https://github.com/cms-sw/framework-team/issues/1148

#### PR validation:

Existing unit tests pass. The ```InputFileCatalog``` unit test was extended to cover the new functions in that class. The other parts of this rely on existing unit tests.

I manually ran a test with simulated input of 100,000 file names and 8 catalogs, then printed out the file names and PFNs. I ran that under MaxMemory tools recently developed and the output is below:

Using version before PR:

```
    Memory Report: total memory requested: 4535444257
    Memory Report:  max memory used: 334632608
    Memory Report:  presently used: 4048
    Memory Report:  # allocations calls:   26410570
    Memory Report:  # deallocations calls: 26410570
```

Using version after PR:

```
    Memory Report: total memory requested: 3702110945
    Memory Report:  max memory used: 21032368
    Memory Report:  presently used: 4048
    Memory Report:  # allocations calls:   19910569
    Memory Report:  # deallocations calls: 19910569
```

The key result is max memory used, improved from 334 MB to 21 MB. There is also a decrease in allocations and total memory requested. This improvement varies based on the number of input file names and catalogs (probably close to being proportional to those things). We have had pile up jobs with more files than that (~500K) and there have been sites with of order 8 catalogs (although 3 or less seems more common in the few I've looked at...). And some fraction of what is going on is related to printing 800000 lines, but the savings in max memory is from the ```InputFileCatalog```  changes and the rest of the test is the same for both cases. The test prints them to force all the PFNs to be calculated.
title: Reduce the memory usage of the InputFileCatalog
